recent_calls.summary: The call was very brief, with the agent attempting to start the conversation by asking for the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to inquire about moving up a routine AC cleaning appointment to an earlier opening available today. The agent confirmed the details and noted the user's flexibility for any time today, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The call ended positively with the user providing a high satisfaction rating.

The user inquired about refrigerant removal for their refrigerator and was informed that service is provided at the user's address, not via drop-off. The user identified themselves and mentioned prior service but declined to provide the service address when asked. The call ended without scheduling service.
The user called to inquire about their apartment and requested to speak with a representative. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

Tyson called to confirm if his appointment was moved to today as he was informed, but the system still showed it for April 7th. The agent confirmed the appointment date and assured Tyson that the team would clarify and follow up with him.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent transferred the call successfully to the requested person.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for customer service assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.

Mark called to reschedule his yearly air conditioning checkup appointment. The agent confirmed the details and booked the appointment for Wednesday, April 8th at 2:00 p.m. Mark declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to reschedule a routine air conditioning check appointment. The agent helped the user select a new time slot for the appointment on March 31, 2026, at 4:00 PM, and the booking was successfully made. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user called to provide an update for Vernon, informing that they are running behind and expect to be home around nine due to dropping off a pet at the vet. The agent acknowledged the update and confirmed passing the message to Vernon. No further assistance was requested.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with customer service. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to report an issue with their doors and requested to speak to a customer service representative. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called and requested to speak with a customer service representative. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.

Deborah called to return a call from Amy and schedule an emergency gas heater service for Monday. The after-hours assistant documented the emergency service request and confirmed the callback number. The call ended with the agent assuring a callback on the next business day.

The user, Ira from American Home Shield, called to provide a dispatch number and request a service appointment for a Fair ForceAir gas furnace that is not working and leaving the home without heat. The user requested the earliest available appointment sooner than two weeks and confirmed the correct service address and contact information. The agent noted all details, offered information about maintenance programs, and received positive feedback from the user.

The call consisted mostly of the user speaking incoherently or off-topic, with the agent repeatedly attempting to engage the user about heating, cooling, or plumbing services. The user did not provide clear information or request any services, and the call ended with the user hanging up.

The user, Deborah, confirmed a service call request for her gas furnace that is not working. She scheduled a regular service appointment for Monday morning with no special instructions. The agent confirmed the details and the user declined to provide feedback.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user, Earl, called to confirm a scheduled plumbing appointment for a water heater issue on Tuesday at 8 AM. The agent confirmed the details and assured Earl that the team would follow up to confirm the appointment. Earl declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user returned a call and mentioned needing service, but indicated that the callback was already about that. The call ended shortly after without further discussion.

James called to inquire about a routine air conditioning checkup scheduled through American Home Shield, expected to happen today. The agent confirmed the service details, address, and contact information, and noted that since it was after-hours, they could not confirm if the technician would arrive today but assured follow-up. James expressed interest in receiving information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the AI assistant.

Christopher Lee called to inquire about warranty coverage for a leak found in his AC unit line during maintenance. The agent confirmed details, discussed available appointment slots, and successfully booked a service appointment for Friday, April 3rd at 8:00 a.m. The user provided positive feedback but wished for more discussion about warranty coverage.

The user called to inquire about the cost of getting a blower fixed in their furnace. The agent explained the standard service fee of $99 for a technician to come out and offered to schedule an appointment. The user confirmed understanding of the fee but did not request a specific appointment time.

Davante called to request a quote for a standard furnace replacement because the current unit was not working. The agent scheduled a provisional appointment for March 31st at 10:00 a.m. and confirmed the details with the user. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user, Earl Lafferty, reported a gurgling sound from his water heater when running the washer and scheduled a service appointment for March 31st at 8:00 a.m. The agent confirmed the details and booked the appointment successfully. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user, Madonna White, called about a thermostat stuck on the heating setting with unresponsive buttons. The agent provided available appointment times for a technician visit but the user decided to call back later to schedule. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and no appointment was booked during the call.

The user, Catrina, called to check on a plumbing service request for an immediate clogged drain issue. The agent confirmed the details and offered available appointment slots, but the user insisted on service today and requested to speak with a live person. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to speak to a live agent to schedule an installation and confirmed that the installation is not related to a home warranty. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Catrina, called to report a completely blocked downstairs sink that is not draining and requested service as soon as possible. The agent confirmed the details, collected contact information, and assured that the service team would reach out to confirm availability. The user provided feedback rating the experience as 4 out of 5, suggesting the agent be a real human.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. The user hung up without responding or engaging further.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any interaction or information exchange, as the agent requested the ZIP code for service address but received no response.

Kevin called to inquire about metal filters that were not put back into his air conditioner after a recent service. The agent confirmed the details, collected the service address, and assured Kevin that the team would review the issue and get back to him on the next business day. The call ended with Kevin acknowledging the information.

The user called to inquire about using their own bottle of Freon for a cooling service repair instead of being charged for the company's Freon. The agent collected all necessary details including job number, service address, user contact information, and noted the urgency for a follow-up. The agent assured the user that the team would review the request and contact them on the next business day.
The user called to inquire about getting a service quote. The agent offered to assist and requested the user's full name to proceed, but the call ended before the user provided it.
The call was very brief and the user hung up immediately after the agent introduced herself. No further conversation or assistance took place.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user did not engage with the agent, and the call ended shortly after it started.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and the user responding with a short greeting before hanging up.

The user, Dale Cobb, called to check if a technician was on the way. The agent informed him that it was after business hours and could not confirm the technician's status but assured a follow-up. The call ended abruptly before the user could finish.

The user, Derek, called to schedule a maintenance visit for his water heater, furnace, and AC. The agent confirmed the details and noted the preferred day as Wednesday, with a follow-up to confirm availability. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback but expressing thanks.

The user, Manikandan, called to inquire about a refrigerant leak in their heating and cooling system and wanted to confirm if a technician could analyze the system with only two pounds of refrigerant. The agent documented the request and informed the user that a live agent would contact them on the next business day for further discussion. The user declined additional assistance and ended the call.

The user, Dwayne Hicks, called to schedule a routine air conditioning tune-up through his American Home Shield plan. The agent confirmed the appointment details for April 1st at 10 AM and noted no emergency or special instructions. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided neutral feedback, preferring to speak with a human representative.

The user called to inquire about an appointment for air conditioner service that was expected between 1 and 3 PM but had not yet occurred. The agent informed the user that appointments cannot be confirmed after hours but assured the team would follow up next business day. The user provided service details, address, and contact information, and requested a callback to schedule the appointment.
The call was initiated by the agent who requested the user's ZIP code to start the service process, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

David called after hours to report that a scheduled heating service appointment was missed and he needs immediate assistance. The agent documented the issue and informed David that the team will follow up on the next business day since live support was unavailable. The call ended with no further requests from David.

The user, Jenny from American Home Sales, called to follow up on a work order for air conditioning service at 13101 Conway Grove Lane, St. Louis. The agent confirmed the details and the preferred callback number, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The call ended with the user agreeing to provide feedback.
